23	// ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind is the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
24	// kind appended to the bead when the post-merge reviewer was routed to the
25	// same provider as the implementer. R4 (different reviewer) is best-effort
26	// emergent: bumping MinPower usually picks a different candidate, but the
27	// guarantee is not absolute. The event surfaces the degradation so operators
28	// can see when reviewer diversity collapsed.
29	const ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind = "review-pairing-degraded"
30	
31	// ReviewerEscalatedEventKind is the kind:reviewer-escalated event kind
32	// appended when the reviewer's MinPower is bumped above the baseline
33	// impl.ActualPower+1 due to prior review-error or review-pairing-degraded
34	// events for the same result_rev.
35	const ReviewerEscalatedEventKind = "reviewer-escalated"
36	
37	// ImplementerRouting captures the implementer's resolved routing details so
38	// the post-merge reviewer can build a paired ExecuteRequest with Role=reviewer,
39	// the same correlation, and MinPower bumped one above the implementer's
40	// actual selected power (R4 pairing).
41	type ImplementerRouting struct {
42		Harness     string
43		Provider    string
44		Model       string
45		ActualPower int
46		// Correlation is the implementer's correlation map. The reviewer copies
47		// its keys into its own dispatch metadata so events / session log /
48		// aggregations can join the two calls.
49		Correlation map[string]string
50	}

55	const (

57		VerdictApprove Verdict = "APPROVE"
58		// VerdictRequestChanges means some AC items need fixing; the bead stays open

181			if strings.HasPrefix(l, prefix) {
182				return true
183			}
184		}
185		return false
186	}

191	}

202		ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error)
203	}

205	// reviewGroupReviewer is the optional pre-close gate used by execute-loop.
206	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements it; test doubles can opt in when they need

212	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
213	// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
214	// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
215	// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
216	// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
217	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
218	
219	For each acceptance-criteria (AC) item, decide whether it is implemented correctly, then assign one overall verdict:
220	
221	- APPROVE — every AC item is fully and correctly implemented.
222	- REQUEST_CHANGES — some AC items are partial or have fixable minor issues.
223	- BLOCK — at least one AC item is not implemented or incorrectly implemented; or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
224	
225	## Required output format (schema_version: 1)
226	
227	Respond with EXACTLY one JSON object as your final response, fenced as a single ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` code block. Do not include any prose outside the fenced block. The JSON must match this schema:
228	
229	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json
230	{
231	  "schema_version": 1,
232	  "verdict": "APPROVE",
233	  "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification",
234	  "per_ac": [
235	    { "number": 1, "item": "acceptance criterion text", "grade": "pass", "evidence": "file:line or test evidence" }
236	  ],
237	  "findings": [
238	    { "severity": "info", "summary": "what is wrong or notable", "location": "path/to/file.go:42" }
239	  ]
240	}
241	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `
242	
243	Rules:
244	- "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK".
245	- "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
246	- Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
247	- Do not echo this template back. Do not write the words APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.`
248	
249	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
250	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
251	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
252	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
253		Caps evidence.Caps
254	}
255	
256	// ReviewPromptOptions controls optional prompt sections.
257	type ReviewPromptOptions struct {
258		IncludeProse bool
259	}
260	
261	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
262	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
263	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
264	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
265	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
266		Prompt   string
267		Overflow bool
268		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
269	}

271	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
272	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
273	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
274	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
275		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
276	}
277	
278	// BuildReviewPromptWithProse builds the review prompt and includes an
279	// advisory prose-quality section derived from the bead text using the same
280	// docprose checker and config path as `ddx doc prose`.
281	func BuildReviewPromptWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
282		return BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
283	}

285	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
286	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
287	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
288	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
289	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
290	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
291	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
292	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
293		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, false)
294	}
295	
296	// BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse assembles the review prompt with the
297	// optional prose-quality section enabled.
298	func BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
299		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, true)
300	}
301	
302	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
303		caps := opts.Caps
304		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
305			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
306		}
307	
308		var sb strings.Builder
309		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
310	
311		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
312	
313		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
314		beadStart := sb.Len()
315		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
316		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
317	
318		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
319			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
320		} else {
321			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
322		}
323	
324		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
325			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
326		} else {
327			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
328		}
329	
330		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
331			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
332		}
333	
334		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
335			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
336		}
337	
338		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
339		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
340			Name:          "bead",
341			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
342			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
343		})
344	
345		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
346		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
347		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
348			invStart := sb.Len()
349			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
350			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
351			for _, f := range allFiles {
352				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
353				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
354			}
355			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
356			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
357				Name:          "changed-files",
358				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
359				SelectedItems: paths,
360			})
361		}
362	
363		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
364		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
365		if len(refs) == 0 {
366			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
367		} else {
368			for _, ref := range refs {
369				if ref.Title != "" {
370					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
371				} else {
372					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
373				}
374				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
375				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
376				if readErr != nil {
377					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
378					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
379						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
380						TruncationReason: "read_error",
381						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
382					})
383				} else {
384					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
385					if truncated {
386						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
387					}
388					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
389					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
390						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
391						BytesIncluded: len(content),
392						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
393					}
394					if truncated {
395						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
396						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
397					}
398					sections = append(sections, sec)
399				}
400				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
401			}
402		}
403		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
404	
405		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
406		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
407		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
408		diffSection.Name = "diff"
409		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
410		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n")))
411	
412		if includeProse {
413			proseSection, proseSectionEvidence, proseErr := buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot, b)
414			if proseErr != nil {
415				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <prose-review advisory=%q>\n", "true")
416				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <note>Could not compute prose findings: %s</note>\n", reviewXMLEscape(proseErr.Error()))
417				sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>\n\n")
418				sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
419					Name:          "prose-review",
420					SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
421				})
422			} else {
423				sb.WriteString(proseSection)
424				sb.WriteString("\n")
425				sections = append(sections, proseSectionEvidence)
426			}
427		}
428	
429		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
430		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
431		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
432		if includeProse {
433			instructions += "\n\nIf a <prose-review> section is present, add a prose_findings array to the JSON output. Keep prose findings separate from correctness findings. Prose findings are advisory by default and must not change the overall verdict."
434		}
435		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
436	
437		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
438	
439		out := sb.String()
440		assembled := evidence.AssembleInline([]evidence.SectionInput{{
441			Name:    "review-prompt",
442			Content: out,
443		}}, len(out))
444		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
445			Prompt:   assembled.Prompt,
446			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
447			Sections: sections,
448		}
449	}
450	
451	func buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead) (string, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection, error) {
452		cfg, err := ddxconfig.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
453		if err != nil {
454			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
455		}
456	
457		settings, err := docprose.ResolveSettings(cfg)
458		if err != nil {
459			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
460		}
461		checker, err := docprose.NewChecker(settings.Mode, settings.Vocabulary)
462		if err != nil {
463			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
464		}
465	
466		var src strings.Builder
467		if title := strings.TrimSpace(b.Title); title != "" {
468			fmt.Fprintf(&src, "# %s\n\n", title)
469		}
470		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
471			src.WriteString(desc)
472			src.WriteString("\n\n")
473		}
474		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
475			src.WriteString("## Acceptance\n\n")
476			src.WriteString(acc)
477			src.WriteString("\n\n")
478		}
479		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
480			src.WriteString("## Notes\n\n")
481			src.WriteString(notes)
482			src.WriteString("\n")
483		}
484	
485		findings := checker.Findings("bead.md", src.String())
486		var sb strings.Builder
487		sb.WriteString("  <prose-review advisory=\"true\">\n")
488		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <mode>%s</mode>\n", settings.Mode)
489		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <policy>%s</policy>\n", settings.Policy)
490		if len(findings) == 0 {
491			sb.WriteString("    <findings/>\n")
492		} else {
493			sb.WriteString("    <findings>\n")
494			for _, finding := range findings {
495				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <finding file=%q line=%d rule_id=%q severity=%q>\n", finding.File, finding.Line, finding.RuleID, finding.Severity)
496				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <rationale>%s</rationale>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.Rationale))
497				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <suggested_edit>%s</suggested_edit>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.SuggestedEdit))
498				sb.WriteString("      </finding>\n")
499			}
500			sb.WriteString("    </findings>\n")
501		}
502		sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>")
503	
504		section := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
505			Name:          "prose-review",
506			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
507		}
508		section.BytesIncluded = len(src.String())
509		return sb.String(), section, nil
510	}
511	
512	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
513	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
514	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
515	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
516	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
517	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
518	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
519		if len(files) == 0 {
520			return originalDiff
521		}
522		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
523			if f.Path == "" {
524				return 3
525			}
526			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
527				return 0
528			}
529			for _, r := range refs {
530				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
531					return 1
532				}
533			}
534			return 2
535		}
536		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
537		copy(ordered, files)
538		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
539	
540		var b2 strings.Builder
541		for _, f := range ordered {
542			body := f.Body
543			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
544				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
545			}
546			b2.WriteString(body)
547		}
548		return b2.String()
549	}
550	
551	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
552	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
553	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
554	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
555	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
556		var b strings.Builder
557		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
558		b.WriteString(f.Path)
559		b.WriteString(" b/")
560		b.WriteString(f.Path)
561		b.WriteString("\n")
562		if f.Stat != "" {
563			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
564			b.WriteString("\n")
565		}
566		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
567			b.WriteString(h)
568			b.WriteString("\n")
569		}
570		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
571		return b.String()
572	}
573	
574	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
575	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
576	// exact symbolic resolution).
577	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
578		if text == "" || p == "" {
579			return false
580		}
581		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
582			return true
583		}
584		base := path.Base(p)
585		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
586	}
587	
588	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
589	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
590	// documents.
591	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
592		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
593			return false
594		}
595		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
596		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
597			return true
598		}
599		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
600			return true
601		}
602		return false
603	}
604	
605	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
606	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
607		var buf bytes.Buffer
608		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
609			"&", "&",
610			"<", "<",
611			">", ">",
612		).Replace(s))
613		return buf.String()
614	}
615	
616	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
617	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
618	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
619	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
620		ProjectRoot string
621		BeadStore   BeadReader
622		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
623		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
624		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
625		Service agentlib.FizeauService
626		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
627		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
628		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
629		Runner AgentRunner
630		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
631		// When Harness is empty, the reviewer follows the implementation harness;
632		// when both are empty, dispatch is left to the agent service.
633		Harness string
634		Model   string
635		// ModelRef is retained for tests/legacy callers that explicitly want an
636		// upstream model-ref override. Default reviewer routing uses fizeau profiles.
637		ModelRef string
638		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
639		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
640		// applies.
641		Caps evidence.Caps
642		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, is used to append the
643		// kind:review-pairing-degraded event when the reviewer's resolved
644		// provider matches the implementer's. Optional: nil disables the
645		// telemetry emission while preserving the rest of the review flow.
646		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
647		// EventReader, when non-nil, is used to count prior review escalation
648		// triggers (review-error and review-pairing-degraded events) for the
649		// same result_rev so the reviewer MinPower is bumped on retry.
650		EventReader BeadEventReader
651	}
652	
653	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
654	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It sets the reviewer harness/profile,
655	// attaches the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid),
656	// and derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
657	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
658	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
659	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
660	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string) AgentRunRuntime {
661		return BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{})
662	}
663	
664	// BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest constructs the reviewer runtime for one
665	// slot in a review group. It threads the implementer's correlation facts,
666	// overlays reviewer role metadata, and stamps the review_group_id and
667	// reviewer_index when provided so downstream routing / tracing can join the
668	// two reviewer slots back to the same evidence bundle.
669	func BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string, meta ReviewGroupDispatchMeta) AgentRunRuntime {
670		correlation := map[string]string{}
671		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
672			correlation[k] = v
673		}
674		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
675		if meta.GroupID != "" {
676			correlation["review_group_id"] = meta.GroupID
677			correlation["reviewer_index"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", meta.ReviewerIndex)
678		}
679		if impl.Harness != "" {
680			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
681		}
682		if impl.Provider != "" {
683			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
684		}
685		if impl.Model != "" {
686			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
687		}
688		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
689			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
690		}
691		minPower := 0
692		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
693			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
694		}
695		correlationID := reviewCorrelationID(correlation)
696		return AgentRunRuntime{
697			HarnessOverride:     reviewerHarness,
698			ProfileOverride:     reviewerProfile,
699			MinPowerOverride:    minPower,
700			Correlation:         correlation,
701			Role:                "reviewer",
702			CorrelationID:       correlationID,
703			PermissionsOverride: PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
704			ClearRoutingPins:    true,
705			ClearProfile:        true,
706			ClearMaxPower:       true,
707		}
708	}
709	
710	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerProfileForDispatch(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting) string {
711		return r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, 0).Name
712	}
713	
714	// reviewerDispatchProfile returns the fizeau profile name and MinPower to use
715	// for the reviewer dispatch. priorErrors is the number of prior review-error
716	// and review-pairing-degraded events for the same result_rev; each adds one
717	// ladder step to MinPower:
718	//   - 0 prior errors: impl.ActualPower+1 (baseline R4 pairing)
719	//   - 1 prior error:  impl.ActualPower+2 (one step above baseline)
720	//   - 2+ prior errors: top-of-ladder (SelectStrongestProfile, no floor)
721	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting, priorErrors int) reviewerDispatchProfile {
722		floor := 0
723		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
724			floor = impl.ActualPower + 1
725		}
726		// Escalated floor: each prior error adds one ladder step.
727		escalatedFloor := floor
728		if priorErrors > 0 && floor > 0 {
729			escalatedFloor = floor + priorErrors
730		}
731		if r.Runner != nil {
732			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
733		}
734		selectedSvc := r.Service
735		if selectedSvc == nil {
736			factory := serviceRunFactory
737			if factory == nil {
738				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
739			}
740			built, err := factory(r.ProjectRoot)
741			if err != nil {
742				return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
743			}
744			selectedSvc = built
745		}
746		snap, err := LoadProfileSnapshot(ctx, selectedSvc)
747		if err != nil {
748			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
749		}
750		// On 2+ prior errors (or 1+ error with unknown impl power), use the
751		// strongest available profile without a floor restriction (top-of-ladder).
752		topOfLadder := priorErrors >= 2 || (priorErrors >= 1 && floor == 0)
753		name := ""
754		if topOfLadder {
755			name = SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
756		} else if escalatedFloor > 0 {
757			name = SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap, escalatedFloor)
758		} else {
759			name = selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap)
760		}
761		if name == "" {
762			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
763		}
764		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
765			if profile.Name == name {
766				// Use the higher of the profile's own MinPower and the escalated floor
767				// so top-of-ladder selection never yields a MinPower below the floor
768				// we computed from the error count.
769				mp := profile.MinPower
770				if escalatedFloor > mp {
771					mp = escalatedFloor
772				}
773				return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: mp}
774			}
775		}
776		return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: escalatedFloor}
777	}
778	
779	func selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap ProfileSnapshot) string {
780		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
781			if profile.Name == "smart" && profileHasAvailableModel(profile, snap.Models) {
782				return profile.Name
783			}
784		}
785		return SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
786	}
787	
788	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) applyExplicitReviewerPins(runtime *AgentRunRuntime) string {
789		if runtime == nil {
790			return ""
791		}
792		if r.Model != "" {
793			runtime.ModelOverride = r.Model
794			return r.Model
795		}
796		if r.ModelRef != "" {
797			runtime.ModelRefOverride = r.ModelRef
798			return r.ModelRef
799		}
800		return runtime.ProfileOverride
801	}
802	
803	func reviewCorrelationID(correlation map[string]string) string {
804		if len(correlation) == 0 {
805			return ""
806		}
807		parts := make([]string, 0, 4)
808		if beadID := correlation["bead_id"]; beadID != "" {
809			parts = append(parts, beadID)
810		}
811		if groupID := correlation["review_group_id"]; groupID != "" {
812			parts = append(parts, groupID)
813		} else if resultRev := correlation["result_rev"]; resultRev != "" {
814			parts = append(parts, resultRev)
815		}
816		if reviewerIndex := correlation["reviewer_index"]; reviewerIndex != "" {
817			parts = append(parts, reviewerIndex)
818		}
819		if len(parts) == 0 {
820			return ""
821		}
822		return strings.Join(parts, ":")
823	}
824	
825	// reviewPairingDegradedBody renders the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
826	// body. The body carries both implementer and reviewer routing details so
827	// operators can see exactly which routes converged. resultRev is included so
828	// the event can be scoped to a specific commit when counting escalation
829	// triggers.
830	func reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProvider, reviewerModel string, reviewerPower int, resultRev string) string {
831		var b strings.Builder
832		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_harness=%s\n", impl.Harness)
833		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_provider=%s\n", impl.Provider)
834		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_model=%s\n", impl.Model)
835		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_actual_power=%d\n", impl.ActualPower)
836		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_harness=%s\n", reviewerHarness)
837		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_provider=%s\n", reviewerProvider)
838		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_model=%s\n", reviewerModel)
839		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_actual_power=%d\n", reviewerPower)
840		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "min_power_requested=%d\n", impl.ActualPower+1)
841		if resultRev != "" {
842			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
843		}
844		return b.String()
845	}
846	
847	// countPriorEscalationTriggers counts review-error and review-pairing-degraded
848	// events recorded against the bead whose body cites the given result_rev. Each
849	// counts as one escalation step — review-error events from transport/overflow/
850	// parse failures and pairing-degraded events from routing collisions both
851	// signal that the current reviewer tier is insufficient.
852	func countPriorEscalationTriggers(reader BeadEventReader, beadID, resultRev string) int {
853		if reader == nil || resultRev == "" {
854			return 0
855		}
856		events, err := reader.Events(beadID)
857		if err != nil {
858			return 0
859		}
860		n := 0
861		for _, ev := range events {
862			if ev.Kind != "review-error" && ev.Kind != ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind {
863				continue
864			}
865			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
866			if m == nil || m[1] != resultRev {
867				continue
868			}
869			n++
870		}
871		return n
872	}
873	
874	// reviewerEscalatedEventBody renders the kind:reviewer-escalated event body.
875	func reviewerEscalatedEventBody(newMinPower, errorCount int, resultRev string) string {
876		var b strings.Builder
877		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "new_min_power=%d\n", newMinPower)
878		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "error_count=%d\n", errorCount)
879		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
880		return b.String()
881	}

883	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
884	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
885		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
886		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
887		if err != nil {
888			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
889		}
890		return r.reviewBeadWithDiff(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl, diff, r.ProjectRoot)
891	}

893	// Review implements CandidateReviewer for the pre-land candidate-cycle path.
894	// It reviews the full base_rev..candidate_rev range and dispatches the reviewer
895	// inside the still-live attempt worktree.
896	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) Review(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, candidate CandidateResult) (CandidateReviewResult, error) {
897		reviewer := *r
898		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
899			reviewer.ProjectRoot = projectRoot
900		}
901		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
902			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: project_root required")
903		}
904		beadID := candidate.Report.BeadID
905		if beadID == "" {
906			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: bead_id required")
907		}
908		diff, err := reviewer.gitDiff(candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev)
909		if err != nil {
910			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: git diff %s..%s: %w", candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev, err)
911		}
912		workDir := candidate.WorktreePath
913		if workDir == "" {
914			workDir = reviewer.ProjectRoot
915		}
916		impl := ImplementerRouting{
917			Harness:     candidate.Report.Harness,
918			Provider:    candidate.Report.Provider,
919			Model:       candidate.Report.Model,
920			ActualPower: candidate.Report.ActualPower,
921			Correlation: map[string]string{
922				"bead_id":       beadID,
923				"attempt_id":    candidate.Report.AttemptID,
924				"session_id":    candidate.Report.SessionID,
925				"result_rev":    candidate.Report.ResultRev,
926				"candidate_ref": candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
927				"cycle_index":   fmt.Sprintf("%d", candidate.CycleIndex),
928			},
929		}
930		group, groupErr := reviewer.reviewGroupWithDiff(ctx, beadID, candidate.Report.ResultRev, impl, diff, workDir)
931		review, reduceErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group)
932		if review == nil {
933			if reduceErr != nil {
934				return CandidateReviewResult{}, reduceErr
935			}
936			return CandidateReviewResult{}, groupErr
937		}
938		out := CandidateReviewResult{
939			Verdict:        string(review.Verdict),
940			Rationale:      review.Rationale,
941			PerAC:          append([]ReviewAC(nil), review.PerAC...),
942			Findings:       append([]Finding(nil), review.Findings...),
943			Classification: ClassifyReviewFindings(review).Class,
944		}
945		if reduceErr != nil {
946			return out, reduceErr
947		}
948		return out, groupErr
949	}
950	
951	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewBeadWithDiff(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting, diff, reviewWorkDir string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
952		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
953		if err != nil {
954			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
955		}
956	
957		// Resolve governing document references.
958		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
959	
960		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
961		iter := 1
962	
963		// Count prior escalation triggers so reviewerDispatchProfile can bump
964		// MinPower on retries.
965		priorErrors := countPriorEscalationTriggers(r.EventReader, beadID, resultRev)
966	
967		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
968		caps := r.Caps
969		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
970			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
971		}
972		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
973		prompt := built.Prompt
974		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
975		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
976		if err != nil {
977			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
978		}
979		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
980			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
981		}
982	
983		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
984		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
985		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
986		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
987		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
988		if built.Overflow {
989			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
990			reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, priorErrors)
991			reviewRouteLabel := reviewProfile.Name
992			if r.Model != "" {
993				reviewRouteLabel = r.Model
994			} else if r.ModelRef != "" {
995				reviewRouteLabel = r.ModelRef
996			}
997			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
998				Sections:    built.Sections,
999				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1000				OutputBytes: 0,
1001				Harness:     r.Harness,
1002				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
1003			}
1004			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1005				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
1006				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1007				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1008				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
1009				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
1010				BaseRev:         baseRev,
1011				ResultRev:       resultRev,
1012				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
1013				CostUSD:         0,
1014				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
1015				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
1016			}
1017			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1018				Harness:          r.Harness,
1019				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
1020				BaseRev:          baseRev,
1021				ResultRev:        resultRev,
1022				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
1023				BeadID:           beadID,
1024				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1025				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
1026			}, reviewArtifactResult{
1027				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
1028				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1029				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
1030			})
1031			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
1032				len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
1033		}
1034	
1035		// Resolve reviewer routing. An explicit reviewer harness/model wins as an
1036		// operator override; otherwise DDx sends only a fizeau profile hint plus
1037		// the reviewer MinPower floor.
1038		reviewHarness := r.Harness
1039		reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, priorErrors)
1040		// Emit reviewer-escalated event when MinPower is bumped above baseline.
1041		if priorErrors > 0 && r.BeadEvents != nil {
1042			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1043				Kind:      ReviewerEscalatedEventKind,
1044				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer escalated to min_power=%d after %d prior error(s)", reviewProfile.MinPower, priorErrors),
1045				Body:      reviewerEscalatedEventBody(reviewProfile.MinPower, priorErrors, resultRev),
1046				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1047				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1048			})
1049		}
1050	
1051		start := time.Now()
1052		runRuntime := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewProfile.Name)
1053		// Apply escalated MinPower: use the higher of the base R4 floor and the
1054		// escalated profile floor so retries reach a stronger reviewer tier.
1055		if reviewProfile.MinPower > runRuntime.MinPowerOverride {
1056			runRuntime.MinPowerOverride = reviewProfile.MinPower
1057		}
1058		reviewRouteLabel := r.applyExplicitReviewerPins(&runRuntime)
1059		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
1060		runRuntime.WorkDir = reviewWorkDir
1061		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
1062	
1063		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
1064		if runErr != nil {
1065			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
1066			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
1067			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
1068			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1069				Sections:    built.Sections,
1070				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1071				OutputBytes: 0,
1072				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
1073				Harness:     reviewHarness,
1074				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
1075			}
1076			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1077				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
1078				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
1079				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
1080				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
1081				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
1082				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
1083				ResultRev:       resultRev,
1084				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
1085				DurationMS:      durationMS,
1086				CostUSD:         resultCost(result),
1087				InputBytes:      transportTelemetry.InputBytes,
1088				OutputBytes:     transportTelemetry.OutputBytes,
1089			}
1090			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1091				Harness:          reviewHarness,
1092				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
1093				BaseRev:          reviewRes.BaseRev,
1094				ResultRev:        resultRev,
1095				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
1096				BeadID:           beadID,
1097				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1098				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1099			}, reviewArtifactResult{
1100				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
1101				Rationale:        reviewRes.Rationale,
1102				Error:            reviewRes.Error,
1103				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1104			})
1105			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
1106		}
1107	
1108		actualHarness := reviewHarness
1109		actualModel := reviewRouteLabel
1110		actualProvider := ""
1111		actualPower := 0
1112		if result != nil {
1113			if result.Harness != "" {
1114				actualHarness = result.Harness
1115			}
1116			if result.Model != "" {
1117				actualModel = result.Model
1118			}
1119			actualProvider = result.Provider
1120			actualPower = result.ActualPower
1121			durationMS = result.DurationMS
1122		}
1123	
1124		output := ""
1125		sessionID := ""
1126		if result != nil {
1127			output = result.Output
1128			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
1129		}
1130		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
1131		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
1132		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
1133		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop leaves the bead open for
1134		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
1135		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
1136		var strictVerdict Verdict
1137		var rationale string
1138		var findings []Finding
1139		if parseErr == nil {
1140			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
1141			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
1142			findings = parsed.Findings
1143			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
1144				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
1145				for _, f := range findings {
1146					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
1147					if line == "" {
1148						continue
1149					}
1150					if f.Location != "" {
1151						line = f.Location + ": " + line
1152					}
1153					parts = append(parts, line)
1154				}
1155				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
1156			}
1157		}
1158		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
1159		telemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1160			Sections:    built.Sections,
1161			InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1162			OutputBytes: len(output),
1163			ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
1164			Harness:     actualHarness,
1165			Model:       actualModel,
1166		}
1167		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1168			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
1169			Rationale:        rationale,
1170			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1171			Findings:         findings,
1172			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1173			RawOutput:        output,
1174			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
1175			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
1176			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
1177			SessionID:        sessionID,
1178			BaseRev:          baseRev,
1179			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1180			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1181			DurationMS:       durationMS,
1182			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
1183			InputBytes:       telemetry.InputBytes,
1184			OutputBytes:      telemetry.OutputBytes,
1185		}
1186	
1187		// R4 pairing-degradation telemetry: when MinPower=actualPower+1 fails
1188		// to push the reviewer onto a different provider than the implementer,
1189		// surface a kind:review-pairing-degraded event so operators can see the
1190		// regression. The result_rev is included in the body so the event can be
1191		// counted as an escalation trigger for subsequent dispatches.
1192		// Best-effort only — emission failures never affect the review outcome.
1193		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
1194			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1195				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
1196				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
1197				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower, resultRev),
1198				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1199				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1200			})
1201		}
1202		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1203			Harness:          actualHarness,
1204			Model:            actualModel,
1205			SessionID:        sessionID,
1206			BaseRev:          baseRev,
1207			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1208			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
1209			BeadID:           beadID,
1210			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1211			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
1212		}, reviewArtifactResult{
1213			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
1214			Rationale:        rationale,
1215			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1216			Findings:         findings,
1217			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1218			Error:            reviewRes.Error,
1219			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
1220		})
1221		if parseErr != nil {
1222			// FEAT-022 §12: distinguish provider_empty (zero bytes) from
1223			// unparseable (text without a recognizable JSON contract). Operators
1224			// triage these differently — provider_empty often signals a context-
1225			// window or backend availability issue, while unparseable signals a
1226			// reviewer-prompt or output-format drift.
1227			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1228			if strings.TrimSpace(output) == "" {
1229				class = evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1230			}
1231			reviewRes.Error = class
1232			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", class, parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
1233		}
1234		return reviewRes, nil
1235	}
1236	
1237	// dispatchReviewRun is a thin SD-024 wrapper around dispatchViaResolvedConfig
1238	// for the post-merge reviewer. The reviewer carries no persistent
1239	// ResolvedConfig of its own — the durable knobs that affect a review
1240	// invocation (timeout, provider, evidence caps) are read from the project's
1241	// .ddx/config.yaml via LoadAndResolve, while reviewer-tier harness/model
1242	// are pinned via the runtime override fields. Resolution order matches the
1243	// execute-bead worker (runner > pre-built service > fresh service).
1244	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
1245		rcfg, _ := ddxconfig.LoadAndResolve(r.ProjectRoot, ddxconfig.CLIOverrides{})
1246		return dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, r.Service, r.Runner, rcfg, runtime)
1247	}
1248	
1249	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
1250	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
1251	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
1252	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
1253	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
1254		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1255		defer cancel()
1256		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1257		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1258		if err != nil {
1259			return "", err
1260		}
1261		return string(out), nil
1262	}
1263	
1264	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitDiff(baseRev, resultRev string) (string, error) {
1265		if strings.TrimSpace(baseRev) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(resultRev) == "" {
1266			return "", fmt.Errorf("base_rev and result_rev are required")
1267		}
1268		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1269		defer cancel()
1270		args := append([]string{"diff", baseRev, resultRev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1271		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1272		if err != nil {
1273			return "", err
1274		}
1275		return string(out), nil
1276	}
1277	
1278	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
1279		if resultRev == "" {
1280			return ""
1281		}
1282		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
1283		if err != nil {
1284			return ""
1285		}
1286		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1287	}
1288	
1289	func writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, manifest reviewArtifactManifest, result reviewArtifactResult) error {
1290		if artifacts == nil {
1291			return nil
1292		}
1293		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ManifestAbs, manifest); err != nil {
1294			return err
1295		}
1296		return writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, result)
1297	}
1298	
1299	func resultCost(result *Result) float64 {
1300		if result == nil {
1301			return 0
1302		}
1303		return result.CostUSD
1304	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead/accheck"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	)
12	
13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}
102

26	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
27	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
28	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
29	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31	// returned to a caller.
32	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
33		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
34		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
35		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
36		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
37		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
38		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA — the candidate revision
39		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
40		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
41		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
42		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
43		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
46		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
47		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
48		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
49	
50		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
51		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
52		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
53		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
54		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
55		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
56		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
57		// OrchestratorStatus is populated after the landing/pre-merge orchestration
58		// decision so result.json can distinguish worker success from final
59		// non-landing outcomes.
60		OrchestratorStatus string `json:"orchestrator_status,omitempty"`
61	
62		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
63		Reason      string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
64		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
65		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
66		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.
67		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
68		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
69		CycleIndex          int               `json:"cycle_index,omitempty"`
70		GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
71		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
72		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
73		// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
74		// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
75		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
76		// generic execution failures.
77		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
78		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
79	
80		// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
81		// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
82		// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
83		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
84	
85		// NoEvidencePaths names worktree paths that remained dirty when the agent
86		// exited without creating a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt. It helps
87		// operators diagnose silent commit failures before the worktree is cleaned up.
88		NoEvidencePaths []string `json:"no_evidence_paths,omitempty"`
89	
90		// ResourceExhausted captures the root and cleanup summary when execution
91		// stopped before a bead attempt because the host could not safely continue.
92		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
93	
94		Harness                     string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
95		Provider                    string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
96		Model                       string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
97		ActualPower                 int     `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
98		PredictedPower              int     `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
99		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64 `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
100		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64 `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
101		PredictedCostSource         string  `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
102		SessionID                   string  `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
103		DurationMS                  int     `json:"duration_ms"`
104		Tokens                      int     `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
105		CostUSD                     float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
106		ExitCode                    int     `json:"exit_code"`
107		Error                       string  `json:"error,omitempty"`
108	
109		// FailureMode classifies why an execution did not land cleanly. Empty
110		// when the bead was merged (task_succeeded landing outcome). Populated
111		// by the orchestrator from known patterns; see ClassifyFailureMode and
112		// the FailureMode* constants in execute_bead_status.go.
113		FailureMode string `json:"failure_mode,omitempty"`
114	
115		ExecutionDir       string             `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
116		PromptFile         string             `json:"prompt_file,omitempty"`
117		ManifestFile       string             `json:"manifest_file,omitempty"`
118		ResultFile         string             `json:"result_file,omitempty"`
119		UsageFile          string             `json:"usage_file,omitempty"`
120		WorktreePath       string             `json:"worktree_path,omitempty"`
121		AttemptDiagnostics *AttemptDiagnostic `json:"attempt_diagnostics,omitempty"`
122		Stderr             string             `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
123		RateLimitBudget    time.Duration      `json:"rate_limit_budget,omitempty"`
124	
125		StartedAt  time.Time `json:"started_at"`
126		FinishedAt time.Time `json:"finished_at"`
127	}

129	// AttemptDiagnostic captures infrastructure state when an attempt cannot be
130	// inspected normally. It is intentionally small and JSON-native so result.json
131	// remains useful even when the worker worktree has already vanished.
132	type AttemptDiagnostic struct {
133		BeadID                 string    `json:"bead_id"`
134		AttemptID              string    `json:"attempt_id"`
135		WorktreePath           string    `json:"worktree_path"`

144	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead.
145	// Implemented by *bead.Store — kept as a minimal interface so the agent
146	// package does not need to import a concrete store type in tests.
147	type BeadEventAppender interface {
148		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
149	}
150	
151	// BeadCancelStore reads/writes the cancel markers on a bead's Extra map.
152	// Implemented by *bead.Store. ADR-022 §Cancel SLA: the worker polls
153	// IsCancelRequested mid-attempt and on a positive read writes
154	// MarkCancelHonored before aborting at the next safe point.
155	type BeadCancelStore interface {
156		IsCancelRequested(id string) (bool, error)
157		MarkCancelHonored(id string) error
158	}
159	
160	// CancelPollInterval is how often the worker checks for an operator cancel
161	// while an agent attempt is in flight. ADR-022 §Cancel SLA names 10s as the
162	// default. Exposed as a variable so tests can override it.
163	var CancelPollInterval = 10 * time.Second
164	
165	// RunStateRefreshInterval controls how often execute-bead refreshes its
166	// per-attempt liveness record while the agent harness is running. Exposed as a
167	// variable so tests can shorten it without slowing production attempts.
168	var RunStateRefreshInterval = 10 * time.Second
169	
170	// ExecuteBeadRuntime carries the non-durable plumbing for an execute-bead
171	// run: per-invocation intent (FromRev, PromptFile, WorkerID) and
172	// non-serializable injection seams (BeadEvents, Service, AgentRunner).
173	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
174	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed
175	// via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's rcfg argument.
176	//
177	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
178	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
179		FromRev         string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
180		PromptFile      string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
181		Output          io.Writer
182		WorkerID        string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
183		BeadEvents      BeadEventAppender
184		BeadCancel      BeadCancelStore // optional: enables operator-cancel mid-attempt poll
185		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
186		Service         agentlib.FizeauService
187		AgentRunner     AgentRunner
188		// RateLimitMaxWait bounds the per-bead total wait spent on rate-limit
189		// retries (ddx-c6e3db02 RateLimitRetryContract / TD-031 §8.4). Zero uses
190		// RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget (5 min). Negative disables the wrapper —
191		// rate-limit responses fall through to the standard execution_failed path.
192		RateLimitMaxWait time.Duration
193	}
194	
195	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
196	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
197	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
198	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
199	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
200	// worktree.
201	type GitOps interface {
202		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
203		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
204		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
205		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
206		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
207		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
208		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
209		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
210		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
211		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
212		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
213		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
214		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
215		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
216		// other work that might prune it.
217		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
218		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
219		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
220		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
221	}

223	// AgentRunner runs an agent with the given options.
224	type AgentRunner interface {
225		Run(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error)
226	}

291	// GoverningRef is the exported alias for executeBeadGoverningRef, for use
292	// outside the agent package (e.g. cmd/bead_review.go).
293	type GoverningRef = executeBeadGoverningRef
294

296		Dir      string `json:"dir"`
297		Prompt   string `json:"prompt"`

300		Checks   string `json:"checks,omitempty"`

304	
305	// Constants for worktree and artifact paths.
306	const (
307		ExecuteBeadWtDir       = ".ddx" // legacy; kept for compatibility
308		ExecuteBeadWtPrefix    = ".execute-bead-wt-"
309		ExecuteBeadArtifactDir = ".ddx/executions"
310	
311		// ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir is the subdirectory under $TMPDIR in which
312		// execute-bead creates its isolated worktrees. Keeping them outside
313		// the project tree prevents child processes (tests, hooks) running
314		// inside the worktree from mutating the parent repository's
315		// .git/config via inherited GIT_DIR.
316		ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir = "ddx-exec-wt"
317	)
318	
319	// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead
320	// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.
321	func executeBeadWorktreePath(projectRoot, beadID, attemptID string) string {
322		base := config.ExecutionWorktreeRoot(projectRoot)
323		if base == "" {
324			base = filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir)
325		}
326		return filepath.Join(base, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)
327	}
328	
329	// RealGitOps implements GitOps via os/exec git commands.
330	type RealGitOps struct{}
331	
332	func (r *RealGitOps) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
333		return r.ResolveRev(dir, "HEAD")
334	}
335	
336	func (r *RealGitOps) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
337		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", rev).Output()
338		if err != nil {
339			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %w", rev, err)
340		}
341		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
342	}
343	
344	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error {
345		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "add", "--detach", wtPath, rev).CombinedOutput()
346		if err != nil {
347			return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
348		}
349		return nil
350	}
351	
352	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error {
353		_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "remove", "--force", wtPath).Run()
354		return nil
355	}
356	
357	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error) {
358		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain").Output()
359		if err != nil {
360			return nil, fmt.Errorf("git worktree list: %w", err)
361		}
362		var paths []string
363		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
364			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "worktree ") {
365				paths = append(paths, strings.TrimPrefix(line, "worktree "))
366			}
367		}
368		return paths, nil
369	}
370	
371	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreePrune(dir string) error {
372		return internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "prune").Run()
373	}
374	
375	// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha via `git update-ref`.
376	func (r *RealGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
377		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
378		if err != nil {
379			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
380		}
381		return nil
382	}
383	
384	// DeleteRef removes ref from dir via `git update-ref -d`.
385	func (r *RealGitOps) DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error {
386		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", "-d", ref).CombinedOutput()
387		if err != nil {
388			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref -d %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
389		}
390		return nil
391	}
392	
393	// IsDirty reports whether dir has any uncommitted changes (tracked modifications or untracked files).
394	func (r *RealGitOps) IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error) {
395		out, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "status", "--porcelain", "--", ".", ":(exclude)"+ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName).Output()
396		return len(bytes.TrimSpace(out)) > 0, nil
397	}
398	
399	func dirtyWorktreePaths(dir string) []string {
400		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "status", "--porcelain", "--untracked-files=all").Output()
401		if err != nil {
402			return nil
403		}
404		var paths []string
405		seen := map[string]bool{}
406		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
407			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
408			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" || len(line) < 4 {
409				continue
410			}
411			path := strings.TrimSpace(line[3:])
412			if idx := strings.Index(path, " -> "); idx >= 0 {
413				path = strings.TrimSpace(path[idx+4:])
414			}
415			if path == "" || seen[path] {
416				continue
417			}
418			seen[path] = true
419			paths = append(paths, path)
420		}
421		return paths
422	}
423	
424	// EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
425	// applied when landEvidence / VerifyCleanWorktree stage the evidence
426	// directory. Only excludes the multi-thousand-line embedded session log
427	// (the single file that actually explodes past provider context
428	// limits). prompt.md, usage.json, manifest.json, and result.json remain
429	// tracked — they are small and serve the audit-trail contract. The
430	// gitignore written by `ddx init` already treats .ddx/executions/*/
431	// embedded as ignored; this pathspec is defence-in-depth for force-add
432	// paths.
433	//
434	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896 — session logs landed as tracked
435	// evidence caused retry prompts to balloon past 2M+ tokens and crash
436	// every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
437	func EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs() []string {
438		return []string{
439			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
440		}
441	}
442	
443	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
444	// applied when review-prompt synthesis runs `git show <rev>` over the
445	// evidence commit. Broader than EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs: excludes
446	// prompt.md and usage.json too, because even though they're tracked for
447	// audit, they're execution-artifact noise from the reviewer's
448	// perspective — the reviewer wants to see the implementation diff, not
449	// the prior attempt's prompt or token counters. This also protects
450	// against old commits (pre-fix) that committed the session log
451	// directly.
452	//
453	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896.
454	func EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs() []string {
455		return []string{
456			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
457			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md",
458			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/usage.json",
459		}
460	}
461	
462	// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes, explicitly excluding harness noise
463	// paths, and creates a commit with msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil)
464	// when a commit was made, (false, nil) when nothing real remained to commit
465	// after exclusions, and (false, err) on failure.
466	func (r *RealGitOps) SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error) {
467		// Do NOT list already-gitignored paths (.ddx/agent-logs, .ddx/workers) as
468		// :(exclude) pathspecs. Git treats a path named by :(exclude) as explicitly
469		// referenced, so when the path is also .gitignored git emits "The following
470		// paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files" AND exits 1 — even
471		// though the pathspec is trying to SKIP it. Paths already in .gitignore are
472		// excluded by default; excludes here are only for paths that would
473		// otherwise be tracked.
474		addArgs := []string{
475			"add", "-A", "--",
476			".",
477		}
478		addArgs = append(addArgs, synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs(dir)...)
479		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, addArgs...).Run(); err != nil {
480			return false, fmt.Errorf("staging changes: %w", err)
481		}
482		statusOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only").Output()
483		if len(bytes.TrimSpace(statusOut)) == 0 {
484			return false, nil
485		}
486		if msg == "" {
487			msg = "chore: execute-bead synthesized result commit"
488		}
489		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "commit", "-m", msg).CombinedOutput()
490		if err != nil {
491			return false, fmt.Errorf("synthesize commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
492		}
493		return true, nil
494	}

496	// checkpointPreDispatchDirt captures caller worktree changes as a commit on
497	// the current branch without using the caller checkout's real index or commit
498	// hooks. This keeps the execute-bead base semantics from FEAT-012 §22 while
499	// avoiding parent-checkout hook failures before the isolated worker worktree
500	// exists.
