22	type reviewRunnerStub struct {
23		result   *Result
24		err      error
25		lastOpts RunArgs
26	}
27	
28	func (r *reviewRunnerStub) Run(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
29		r.lastOpts = opts
30		return r.result, r.err
31	}
32	
33	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	// SelectReviewerTier
35	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	func TestSelectReviewerTier_AlwaysSmart(t *testing.T) {
38		assert.Equal(t, escalation.TierSmart, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierCheap))
39		assert.Equal(t, escalation.TierSmart, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierStandard))
40		assert.Equal(t, escalation.TierSmart, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
41	}
42	
43	func TestPreLandReview_UsesAttemptWorktree(t *testing.T) {
44		projectRoot, baseRev, store := newReviewArtifactsFixture(t)
45		resultRev := commitReviewFixtureFile(t, projectRoot, "candidate.txt", "candidate change\n")
46		attemptWorktree := t.TempDir()
47		runner := &reviewRunnerStub{result: approveReviewResult()}
48		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{
49			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
50			BeadStore:   store,
51			Runner:      runner,
52		}
53	
54		res, err := reviewer.Review(context.Background(), projectRoot, CandidateResult{
55			Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
56				BeadID:    "ddx-review-happy",
57				AttemptID: "attempt-preland-worktree",
58				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
59				BaseRev:   baseRev,
60				ResultRev: resultRev,
61			},
62			WorktreePath: attemptWorktree,
63		})
64		require.NoError(t, err)
65		assert.Equal(t, "APPROVE", res.Verdict)
66		assert.Equal(t, attemptWorktree, runner.lastOpts.WorkDir)
67	}
68	
69	func TestPreLandReview_ReadOnlyProfileRequired(t *testing.T) {
70		projectRoot, baseRev, store := newReviewArtifactsFixture(t)
71		resultRev := commitReviewFixtureFile(t, projectRoot, "candidate.txt", "candidate change\n")
72		runner := &reviewRunnerStub{result: approveReviewResult()}
73		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{
74			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
75			BeadStore:   store,
76			Runner:      runner,
77		}
78	
79		_, err := reviewer.Review(context.Background(), projectRoot, CandidateResult{
80			Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
81				BeadID:    "ddx-review-happy",
82				AttemptID: "attempt-preland-readonly",

84				BaseRev:   baseRev,
85				ResultRev: resultRev,
86			},
87			WorktreePath: t.TempDir(),
88		})
89		require.NoError(t, err)
90		assert.Equal(t, PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer, runner.lastOpts.Permissions)
91		assert.Equal(t, "reviewer", runner.lastOpts.Role)
92	}
93	
94	func TestPreLandReview_DiffCoversBaseToCandidate(t *testing.T) {
95		projectRoot, baseRev, store := newReviewArtifactsFixture(t)
96		_ = commitReviewFixtureFile(t, projectRoot, "first.txt", "first candidate line\n")
97		resultRev := commitReviewFixtureFile(t, projectRoot, "second.txt", "second candidate line\n")
98		runner := &reviewRunnerStub{result: approveReviewResult()}
99		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{
100			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
101			BeadStore:   store,
102			Runner:      runner,
103		}
104	
105		_, err := reviewer.Review(context.Background(), projectRoot, CandidateResult{
106			Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
107				BeadID:    "ddx-review-happy",
108				AttemptID: "attempt-preland-diff",
109				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
110				BaseRev:   baseRev,
111				ResultRev: resultRev,
112			},
113			WorktreePath: t.TempDir(),
114		})
115		require.NoError(t, err)
116		assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, "first candidate line", "pre-land review must cover the full base..candidate range")
117		assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, "second candidate line")
118		assert.NotContains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, "commit "+resultRev, "candidate review must not use single-commit git show output")
119	}
120	
121	func approveReviewResult() *Result {
122		return &Result{
123			Harness:    "claude",
124			Model:      "claude-opus-4-6",
125			Output:     `{"schema_version":1,"verdict":"APPROVE","summary":"ok to land","per_ac":[{"number":1,"item":"AC#1","grade":"pass","evidence":"candidate diff reviewed"}]}`,
126			DurationMS: 10,
127		}
128	}
129	
130	func commitReviewFixtureFile(t *testing.T, projectRoot, name, body string) string {
131		t.Helper()
132		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, name), []byte(body), 0o644))
133		out, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "add", name).CombinedOutput()
134		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
135		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@example.com", "commit", "-m", "candidate "+name).CombinedOutput()
136		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
137		headRaw, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
138		require.NoError(t, err)
139		return strings.TrimSpace(string(headRaw))
140	}

162	// makeReviewer builds a beadReviewerFunc that always returns the given verdict.
163	func makeReviewer(verdict Verdict, output string) beadReviewerFunc {
164		return beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
165			res := &ReviewResult{
166				Verdict:         verdict,
167				RawOutput:       output,
168				ReviewerHarness: "claude",
169				ReviewerModel:   "claude-opus-4-6",
170			}
171			if verdict == VerdictApprove {
172				res.PerAC = []ReviewAC{
173					{
174						Number:   1,
175						Item:     "AC#1",
176						Evidence: "evidence-backed approval",
177					},
178				}
179			}
180			return res, nil
181		})
182	}
183	
184	func makeReviewerWithCost(verdict Verdict, output string, costUSD float64) beadReviewerFunc {
185		return beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
186			res := &ReviewResult{
187				Verdict:         verdict,
188				RawOutput:       output,
189				ReviewerHarness: "claude",
190				ReviewerModel:   "claude-opus-4-6",
191				CostUSD:         costUSD,
192			}
193			if verdict == VerdictApprove {
194				res.PerAC = []ReviewAC{
195					{
196						Number:   1,
197						Item:     "AC#1",
198						Evidence: "evidence-backed approval",
199					},
200				}
201			}
202			return res, nil
203		})
204	}

249	func TestRunPostMergeReviewChargesReviewCostAndDefersWhenCapTrips(t *testing.T) {
250		cases := []struct {
251			name         string
252			maxCost      float64
253			preCost      float64
254			reviewCost   float64
255			wantDeferred bool
256			wantApproved bool
257		}{
258			{
259				name:         "normal fit",
260				maxCost:      10.0,
261				preCost:      4.0,
262				reviewCost:   1.5,
263				wantDeferred: false,
264				wantApproved: true,
265			},
266			{
267				name:         "review tips over budget",
268				maxCost:      5.0,
269				preCost:      4.0,
270				reviewCost:   1.5,
271				wantDeferred: true,
272				wantApproved: false,
273			},
274		}
275		for _, tc := range cases {
276			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
277				store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
278				tracker := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(tc.maxCost, func(string) bool { return true })
279				tracker.Add("openrouter", tc.preCost)
280	
281				reviewer := makeReviewerWithCost(VerdictApprove, "### Verdict: APPROVE\n\nAll good.", tc.reviewCost)
282				out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{
283					Bead: *first,
284					Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
285						BeadID:    first.ID,
286						Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
287						SessionID: "sess-review-cost",
288						ResultRev: "cafebabe",
289						CostUSD:   tc.preCost,
290					},
291					Reviewer:      reviewer,
292					Store:         store,
293					ProjectRoot:   t.TempDir(),
294					Rcfg:          config.NewTestConfigForLoop(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"})),
295					Now:           time.Now,
296					Assignee:      "worker",
297					ReviewCostCap: tracker,
298				})
299				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantApproved, out.Approved)
300				require.NoError(t, out.StoreErr)
301				assert.InDelta(t, tc.preCost+tc.reviewCost, tracker.Spent(), 1e-9)
302	
303				events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
304				require.NoError(t, err)
305				foundDeferred := false
306				for _, ev := range events {
307					if ev.Kind == "review-cost-deferred" {
308						foundDeferred = true
309						assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, "result_rev=cafebabe")
310					}
311				}
312				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantDeferred, foundDeferred)
313			})
314		}
315	}

606		out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{
607			Bead: *first,
608			Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
609				BeadID:    first.ID,
610				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,

612				ResultRev: "cafed00d",
613			},

619			Assignee:    "worker",
620		})

622		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction, out.Report.Status)
623	
624		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
625		require.NoError(t, err)
626		// ddx-e30e60a9 + ddx-738edf47: malformed BLOCK is a reviewer malfunction,
627		// not a terminal verdict. The loop refuses to close on a malfunction so
628		// a later attempt can retry. Pre-wave-2 behavior closed eagerly before
629		// review and left the malformed-BLOCK bead closed — that was the silent
630		// false-closure surface these beads eliminate.
631		assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
632			"malformed BLOCK must not close the bead — closing on reviewer malfunction was the silent-false-closure shape")
633		assert.NotContains(t, got.Notes, "REVIEW:BLOCK")
634	}

665				Output:         "```json\n{\"schema_version\":1,\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\",\"summary\":\"AC#3 regression test missing\",\"findings\":[{\"severity\":\"block\",\"summary\":\"regression test missing\",\"location\":\"file.go:42\"}]}\n```",

672		res, err := reviewer.ReviewBead(context.Background(), "ddx-review-artifacts", head, ImplementerRouting{Harness: "claude", Model: "claude-sonnet"})
673		require.NoError(t, err)
674		require.NotNil(t, res)
675		assert.Equal(t, VerdictBlock, res.Verdict)
676		assert.Equal(t, "AC#3 regression test missing", res.Rationale)
677		assert.InDelta(t, 0.0314, res.CostUSD, 1e-9)
678		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir)
679	
680		promptPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(res.ExecutionDir), "prompt.md")
681		manifestPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(res.ExecutionDir), "manifest.json")
682		resultPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(res.ExecutionDir), "result.json")
683		for _, path := range []string{promptPath, manifestPath, resultPath} {
684			_, err := os.Stat(path)
685			require.NoError(t, err, "expected review artifact %s", path)
686		}
687	
688		rawResult, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
689		require.NoError(t, err)
690		var artifactResult reviewArtifactResult
691		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rawResult, &artifactResult))
692		assert.Equal(t, string(VerdictBlock), artifactResult.Verdict)
693		assert.Equal(t, "AC#3 regression test missing", artifactResult.Rationale)
694	
695		var manifest reviewArtifactManifest
696		rawManifest, err := os.ReadFile(manifestPath)
697		require.NoError(t, err)
698		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rawManifest, &manifest))
699		assert.Equal(t, "native-review-1", manifest.SessionID)
700		assert.Equal(t, strings.TrimSpace(head), manifest.ResultRev)
701	}

834	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier verifies the retired post-land
835	// reviewer is unreachable from execute-loop success. Candidate-cycle review
836	// owns repair/retry now, so a success closes directly within one worker.Run.
837	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier(t *testing.T) {
838		// Use a single-bead store so we can assert "exactly one attempt".
839		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
840		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
841		only := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-bound-1", Title: "Impossible AC"}
842		require.NoError(t, store.Create(only))
843	
844		executorCalls := 0
845		reviewerCalls := 0
846		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
847			Store: store,
848			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
849				executorCalls++
850				return ExecuteBeadReport{
851					BeadID:    beadID,
852					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
853					SessionID: "sess-bound",
854					ResultRev: "11111111",
855				}, nil
856			}),
857			Reviewer: beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
858				reviewerCalls++
859				return &ReviewResult{Verdict: VerdictRequestChanges}, nil
860			}),
861		}
862	
863		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
864		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
865		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
866			// no Once flag: drain the queue fully within this run
867		})
868		require.NoError(t, err)
869	
870		// The executor should have been called exactly once; the retired
871		// post-land review path should not run or trigger an in-loop retry.
872		assert.Equal(t, 1, executorCalls, "bead should be attempted exactly once within a single worker.Run call")
873		assert.Equal(t, 0, reviewerCalls, "legacy post-land reviewer must not run from execute-loop success")
874		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
875		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
876	
877		got, err := store.Get(only.ID)
878		require.NoError(t, err)
879		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
880	}

907	// TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff is the regression for
908	// ddx-39e27896. A prior attempt that tracked a multi-thousand-line
909	// session log (.ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl) in git
910	// history would cause DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow to emit a <diff>
911	// section sized O(session log), pushing retry prompts past 2M tokens
912	// and crashing every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
913	//
914	// This test creates a synthetic repo matching the failure scenario —
915	// a commit that adds a 10k-line embedded session log — then runs
916	// gitShow and asserts the output excludes the embedded file content
917	// and stays bounded.
918	func TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff(t *testing.T) {
919		root := t.TempDir()
920		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
921		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
922		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
923	
924		// Seed commit so we have a base rev.
925		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "README.md"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
926			t.Fatalf("write seed: %v", err)
927		}
928		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "README.md")
929		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "seed")
930	
931		// Synthetic evidence commit that adds a multi-thousand-line session log
932		// PLUS a legitimate implementation change. The fix must exclude the
933		// session log from the diff while keeping the implementation change.
934		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "executions", "20260417T000000-testattempt", "embedded")
935		if err := os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
936			t.Fatalf("mkdir evidence: %v", err)
937		}
938		var bigLog strings.Builder
939		for i := 0; i < 10000; i++ {
940			fmt.Fprintf(&bigLog, "{\"seq\":%d,\"event\":\"tool_call\",\"payload\":\"lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipiscing elit\"}\n", i)
941		}
942		sessionLogPath := filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "agent-123.jsonl")
943		if err := os.WriteFile(sessionLogPath, []byte(bigLog.String()), 0o644); err != nil {
944			t.Fatalf("write session log: %v", err)
945		}
946	
947		// Legitimate implementation change that MUST survive in the diff.
948		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "implementation.go"), []byte("package main\n\nfunc Added() {}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
949			t.Fatalf("write implementation: %v", err)
950		}
951	
952		// Force-add the evidence (the pre-fix landEvidence behavior) plus the real change.
953		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "-f", ".ddx/executions/")
954		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "implementation.go")
955		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: add execution evidence [testattempt] + impl")
956	
957		// Get the HEAD sha (the evidence commit).
958		headSha := strings.TrimSpace(runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD"))
959	
960		// Call the gitShow method with the fix in place.
961		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{ProjectRoot: root}
962		out, err := reviewer.gitShow(headSha)
963		if err != nil {
964			t.Fatalf("gitShow: %v", err)
965		}
966	
967		// Must NOT include the session log content.
968		if strings.Contains(out, "lorem ipsum dolor sit amet") {
969			t.Errorf("gitShow output includes embedded session log content (pathspec exclusion not applied)")
970		}
971	
972		// Must include the legitimate implementation change.
973		if !strings.Contains(out, "implementation.go") {
974			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the legitimate implementation file (pathspec too aggressive)")
975		}
976		if !strings.Contains(out, "func Added()") {
977			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the implementation change body")
978		}
979	
980		// Must be bounded in size. The raw session log was ~1MB; fixed diff
981		// should be under 50KB easily (seed + impl.go + evidence metadata).
982		if len(out) > 150_000 {
983			t.Errorf("gitShow output size %d exceeds 150KB budget — pathspec exclusion did not bound the diff", len(out))
984		}
985	}

26	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
27	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
28	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
29	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31	// returned to a caller.
32	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
33		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
34		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
35		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
36		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
37		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
38		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA — the candidate revision
39		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
40		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
41		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
42		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
43		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
46		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
47		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
48		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
49	
50		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
51		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
52		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
53		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
54		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
55		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
56		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
57		// OrchestratorStatus is populated after the landing/pre-merge orchestration
58		// decision so result.json can distinguish worker success from final
59		// non-landing outcomes.
60		OrchestratorStatus string `json:"orchestrator_status,omitempty"`
61	
62		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
63		Reason      string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
64		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
65		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
66		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.
67		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
68		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
69		CycleIndex          int               `json:"cycle_index,omitempty"`
70		GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
71		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
72		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
73		// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
74		// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
75		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
76		// generic execution failures.
77		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
78		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
79	
80		// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
81		// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
82		// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
83		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
84	
85		// NoEvidencePaths names worktree paths that remained dirty when the agent
86		// exited without creating a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt. It helps
87		// operators diagnose silent commit failures before the worktree is cleaned up.
88		NoEvidencePaths []string `json:"no_evidence_paths,omitempty"`
89	
90		// ResourceExhausted captures the root and cleanup summary when execution
91		// stopped before a bead attempt because the host could not safely continue.
92		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
93	
94		Harness                     string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
95		Provider                    string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
96		Model                       string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
97		ActualPower                 int     `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
98		PredictedPower              int     `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
99		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64 `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
100		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64 `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
101		PredictedCostSource         string  `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
102		SessionID                   string  `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
103		DurationMS                  int     `json:"duration_ms"`
104		Tokens                      int     `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
105		CostUSD                     float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
106		ExitCode                    int     `json:"exit_code"`
107		Error                       string  `json:"error,omitempty"`
108	
109		// FailureMode classifies why an execution did not land cleanly. Empty
110		// when the bead was merged (task_succeeded landing outcome). Populated
111		// by the orchestrator from known patterns; see ClassifyFailureMode and
112		// the FailureMode* constants in execute_bead_status.go.
113		FailureMode string `json:"failure_mode,omitempty"`
114	
115		ExecutionDir       string             `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
116		PromptFile         string             `json:"prompt_file,omitempty"`
117		ManifestFile       string             `json:"manifest_file,omitempty"`
118		ResultFile         string             `json:"result_file,omitempty"`
119		UsageFile          string             `json:"usage_file,omitempty"`
120		WorktreePath       string             `json:"worktree_path,omitempty"`
121		AttemptDiagnostics *AttemptDiagnostic `json:"attempt_diagnostics,omitempty"`
122		Stderr             string             `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
123		RateLimitBudget    time.Duration      `json:"rate_limit_budget,omitempty"`
124	
125		StartedAt  time.Time `json:"started_at"`
126		FinishedAt time.Time `json:"finished_at"`
127	}
128	
129	// AttemptDiagnostic captures infrastructure state when an attempt cannot be
130	// inspected normally. It is intentionally small and JSON-native so result.json
131	// remains useful even when the worker worktree has already vanished.
132	type AttemptDiagnostic struct {
133		BeadID                 string    `json:"bead_id"`
134		AttemptID              string    `json:"attempt_id"`
135		WorktreePath           string    `json:"worktree_path"`
136		HeadRevError           string    `json:"head_rev_error,omitempty"`

### 1. **cli/internal/bead/accheck/** Directory

**Files in this directory:**

**accheck.go overview:**
This package provides mechanical verification of acceptance criteria (AC) against the working tree and diff. Key components:

- **Kind classification** (6 types):
  - `KindTestName` - Go test identifiers
  - `KindBuildGate` - lefthook/pre-commit/test gates
  - `KindNegative` - absence/removal assertions
  - `KindSymbol` - backtick-delimited identifiers
  - `KindMechanical` - renames/file moves/docs
  - `KindProse` - free-form text (always needs_judgment)

- **Result enum** (4 states):
  - `ResultPass`, `ResultFail`, `ResultNeedsJudgment`, `ResultError`

- **Main functions:**
  - `ParseAcceptance()` - splits AC into numbered items (1., 2., etc. or (a), (b))
  - `classify()` - determines Kind and extracts entity names
  - `Evaluate()` - resolves each item via mechanical evaluators
  - `evaluateOne()` - dispatches to kind-specific evaluator
  - Various evaluators: `evalTestName()`, `evalSymbol()`, `evalNegative()`, `evalBuildGate()`, `evalMechanical()`
  - `Aggregate()` - builds final Output with summary counts
  - Default hook providers: `DefaultGitGrep()`, `DefaultDiffHits()`, `DefaultRunTest()`

**Key insight:** Prose AC never auto-pass or auto-fail; they always require reviewer judgment.

---

1551	// the lock is not re-entrant.

1554			return commitTrackerLocked(projectRoot)

1564	func commitTrackerLocked(projectRoot string) error {

1572		}
1573		if info.IsDir() {
1574			return nil
1575		}

1580		}

1585		// committed the tracker changes between our pre-lock check and now.

1588			return fmt.Errorf("checking tracker diff: %w", err)
1589		}
1590		if strings.TrimSpace(string(diff)) == "" {

1592			if err != nil {

1594			}
1595			if strings.TrimSpace(string(untracked)) == "" {
1596				return nil
1597			}
1598		}
1599	
1600		commitOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
1601		if err != nil {
1602			return fmt.Errorf("staging tracker: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
1603		}

424	// EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
425	// applied when landEvidence / VerifyCleanWorktree stage the evidence
426	// directory. Only excludes the multi-thousand-line embedded session log
427	// (the single file that actually explodes past provider context
428	// limits). prompt.md, usage.json, manifest.json, and result.json remain
429	// tracked — they are small and serve the audit-trail contract. The
430	// gitignore written by `ddx init` already treats .ddx/executions/*/
431	// embedded as ignored; this pathspec is defence-in-depth for force-add
432	// paths.
433	//
434	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896 — session logs landed as tracked
435	// evidence caused retry prompts to balloon past 2M+ tokens and crash
436	// every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
437	func EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs() []string {
438		return []string{
439			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
440		}
441	}
442	
443	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
444	// applied when review-prompt synthesis runs `git show <rev>` over the
445	// evidence commit. Broader than EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs: excludes
446	// prompt.md and usage.json too, because even though they're tracked for
447	// audit, they're execution-artifact noise from the reviewer's
448	// perspective — the reviewer wants to see the implementation diff, not
449	// the prior attempt's prompt or token counters. This also protects
450	// against old commits (pre-fix) that committed the session log
451	// directly.
452	//
453	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896.
454	func EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs() []string {
455		return []string{
456			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
457			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md",
458			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/usage.json",
459		}
460	}

462	// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes, explicitly excluding harness noise
463	// paths, and creates a commit with msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil)
464	// when a commit was made, (false, nil) when nothing real remained to commit
465	// after exclusions, and (false, err) on failure.

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"

14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/triage"
16	)
17	
18	// triageDecisionEventKind is the event kind used to record the triage
19	// policy's chosen action after a non-APPROVE review verdict.
20	const triageDecisionEventKind = "triage-decision"
21	
22	// PostMergeReviewInput bundles the data RunPostMergeReview needs from its
23	// caller — historically the execute-bead loop body, now the per-attempt
24	// pipeline owned by try.Attempt (ddx-a921ff01 C3). The fields mirror what
25	// the inlined block read from the worker, the candidate bead, the report,
26	// and the loop runtime.
27	type PostMergeReviewInput struct {
28		Bead          bead.Bead
29		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
30		Reviewer      BeadReviewer
31		Store         ExecuteBeadLoopStore
32		ProjectRoot   string
33		Rcfg          config.ResolvedConfig
34		NoReview      bool
35		Log           io.Writer
36		Assignee      string
37		Now           func() time.Time
38		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
39	}
40	
41	// PostMergeReviewOutput carries the review state machine's decision back to
42	// the caller. Report is the (possibly updated) ExecuteBeadReport; Approved
43	// reports whether the caller should treat this attempt as a success. When
44	// the review path encountered a non-fatal Store error the caller is expected
45	// to route StoreErr through its outcome-error handler
46	// (handleOutcomeStoreError on ExecuteBeadWorker), keyed by StoreErrOp.
47	//
48	// RepairContext is non-nil when the BLOCK verdict was classified as a fixable
49	// gap and exactly one repair cycle has been scheduled for this result_rev.
50	// The caller should run a repair attempt with MinPower above
51	// RepairContext.ImplementerActualPower while preserving all explicit
52	// harness/model/provider/profile pins from the original attempt.
53	type PostMergeReviewOutput struct {
54		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
55		Approved      bool
56		StoreErrOp    string
57		StoreErr      error
58		RepairContext *RepairContextFromReviewGroup
59	}
60	
61	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
62	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer or --no-review)
63	// the bead is closed and the function returns Approved=true. When review is
64	// active, the execute-loop asks the review coordinator to evaluate the
65	// close-eligible result before CloseWithEvidence runs; close only proceeds on
66	// unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE. REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK record their
67	// review events without reopening the bead.
68	//
69	// The review:skip label only skips when it carries a sibling
70	// review:skip-reason:* label; otherwise it is ignored. When review is not
71	// skipped, the review coordinator's verdict(s) or error drive the canonical
72	// event emissions (review / review-error / review-manual-required /
73	// review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict the triage policy is
74	// still consulted without reopening the bead.
75	//
76	// Returns Approved=true on the APPROVE path and on the skip path; returns
77	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed APPROVE/BLOCK, or
78	// reviewer transport error. Store errors are surfaced via StoreErrOp +
79	// StoreErr so callers can keep the existing handleOutcomeStoreError pattern
80	// unchanged.
81	//
82	// This is the C3 (ddx-a921ff01) extraction of the 180-line review block
83	// previously inline in execute_bead_loop.go; the loop now sees a structured
84	// outcome and conflict-recovery / decomposition / no_changes paths remain
85	// in the loop body until subsequent C-series beads (C4, C5) move them too.
86	func RunPostMergeReview(ctx context.Context, in PostMergeReviewInput) PostMergeReviewOutput {
87		report := in.Report
88		out := PostMergeReviewOutput{Report: report, Approved: true}
89	
90		now := in.Now
91		if now == nil {
92			now = time.Now
93		}
94	
95		reviewSkipped := in.Reviewer == nil || in.NoReview
96		if !reviewSkipped && HasBeadLabel(in.Bead.Labels, "review:skip") {
97			reviewSkipped = HasBeadLabelPrefix(in.Bead.Labels, "review:skip-reason:")
98		}
99	
100		if reviewSkipped {
101			if err := in.Store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
102				out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
103				out.StoreErr = err
104				return out
105			}
106			out.Report = report
107			return out
108		}
109	
110		// AC 1: Pre-dispatch cost cap check — enforce max_billable_usd before each
111		// adversarial reviewer invocation. If the drain-level budget is already
112		// exhausted, classify as review-error: cost_cap_exceeded and feed the
113		// review_max_retries / review-manual-required retry path so the bead is
114		// left open and not silently closed.
115		if capTracker := in.ReviewCostCap; capTracker != nil {
116			if detail, capped := capTracker.Tripped(); capped {
117				class := evidence.OutcomeReviewCostCapExceeded
118				prior := CountPriorReviewErrors(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev)
119				attemptCount := prior + 1
120				maxRetries := in.Rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()

907	// TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff is the regression for
908	// ddx-39e27896. A prior attempt that tracked a multi-thousand-line
909	// session log (.ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl) in git
910	// history would cause DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow to emit a <diff>
911	// section sized O(session log), pushing retry prompts past 2M tokens
912	// and crashing every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
913	//
914	// This test creates a synthetic repo matching the failure scenario —
915	// a commit that adds a 10k-line embedded session log — then runs
916	// gitShow and asserts the output excludes the embedded file content
917	// and stays bounded.
918	func TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff(t *testing.T) {
919		root := t.TempDir()
920		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
921		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
922		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
923	
924		// Seed commit so we have a base rev.
925		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "README.md"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
926			t.Fatalf("write seed: %v", err)
927		}
928		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "README.md")
929		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "seed")
930	
931		// Synthetic evidence commit that adds a multi-thousand-line session log
932		// PLUS a legitimate implementation change. The fix must exclude the
933		// session log from the diff while keeping the implementation change.
934		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "executions", "20260417T000000-testattempt", "embedded")
935		if err := os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
936			t.Fatalf("mkdir evidence: %v", err)
937		}
938		var bigLog strings.Builder
939		for i := 0; i < 10000; i++ {
940			fmt.Fprintf(&bigLog, "{\"seq\":%d,\"event\":\"tool_call\",\"payload\":\"lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipiscing elit\"}\n", i)
941		}
942		sessionLogPath := filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "agent-123.jsonl")
943		if err := os.WriteFile(sessionLogPath, []byte(bigLog.String()), 0o644); err != nil {
944			t.Fatalf("write session log: %v", err)
945		}
946	
947		// Legitimate implementation change that MUST survive in the diff.
948		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "implementation.go"), []byte("package main\n\nfunc Added() {}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
949			t.Fatalf("write implementation: %v", err)
950		}
951	
952		// Force-add the evidence (the pre-fix landEvidence behavior) plus the real change.
953		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "-f", ".ddx/executions/")
954		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "implementation.go")
955		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: add execution evidence [testattempt] + impl")
956	
957		// Get the HEAD sha (the evidence commit).
958		headSha := strings.TrimSpace(runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD"))
959	
960		// Call the gitShow method with the fix in place.
961		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{ProjectRoot: root}
962		out, err := reviewer.gitShow(headSha)
963		if err != nil {
964			t.Fatalf("gitShow: %v", err)
965		}
966	
967		// Must NOT include the session log content.
968		if strings.Contains(out, "lorem ipsum dolor sit amet") {
969			t.Errorf("gitShow output includes embedded session log content (pathspec exclusion not applied)")
970		}
971	
972		// Must include the legitimate implementation change.
973		if !strings.Contains(out, "implementation.go") {
974			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the legitimate implementation file (pathspec too aggressive)")
975		}
976		if !strings.Contains(out, "func Added()") {
977			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the implementation change body")
978		}
979	
980		// Must be bounded in size. The raw session log was ~1MB; fixed diff
981		// should be under 50KB easily (seed + impl.go + evidence metadata).
982		if len(out) > 150_000 {
983			t.Errorf("gitShow output size %d exceeds 150KB budget — pathspec exclusion did not bound the diff", len(out))
984		}
985	}

310		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"

314	
315		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
316		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
317		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
318		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
319		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
320		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
321		//
322		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
323		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
324		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
325		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
326		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
327		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
328	
329		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
330		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
331		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
332		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
333		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"

336		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
337		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
338		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
339		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
340	
341		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired is set when the focused
342		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
343		// operator judgment, not automated retry. The bead moves to status=proposed
344		// with a kind:land-conflict-operator-required event.
345		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired = "land_conflict_operator_required"
346	
347		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock is set when a BLOCK review verdict
348		// is classified as a terminal operator-required condition —
349		// review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth
350		// cap, or review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope. These classes do not re-enter
351		// the automated retry cycle and do not consume the no-progress retry
352		// budget; the bead moves to status=proposed until an operator
353		// intervenes.
354		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock = "review_terminal_block"

359		// Exactly one repair cycle is scheduled per result_rev; a second
360		// review_fixable_gap on the same result_rev falls through to the regular

371	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
372	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
373	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
374	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
375		if exitCode != 0 {
376			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
377		}
378	
379		switch outcome {
380		case "merged":
381			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
382			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
383			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
384			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
385			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
386			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
387				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
388			}
389			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {

1	package bead
2	
3	import "fmt"
4	
5	// ParkReason identifies the canonical reason for transitioning a bead to
6	// proposed status for operator attention. Each constant maps to a fixed
7	// (Reason, Source) pair used by ParkToProposed.
8	type ParkReason string
9	
10	const (
11		ParkIntakeRejection           ParkReason = "intake_rejection"
12		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired ParkReason = "no_changes_operator_required"
13		ParkPostReviewMalfunction     ParkReason = "post_review_malfunction"
14		ParkReviewTerminal            ParkReason = "review_terminal"
15		ParkConflictRecovery          ParkReason = "conflict_recovery"
16	)
17	
18	type parkReasonMeta struct {
19		Reason string
20		Source string
21	}
22	
23	var parkReasonMetaMap = map[ParkReason]parkReasonMeta{
24		ParkIntakeRejection:           {Reason: "pre-claim intake blocked execution", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
25		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired: {Reason: "operator decision required before another automated attempt", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
26		ParkPostReviewMalfunction:     {Reason: "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
27		ParkReviewTerminal:            {Reason: "terminal review block requires operator decision", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
28		ParkConflictRecovery:          {Reason: "land conflict requires operator judgment", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
29	}
30	
31	// ParkToProposed transitions the bead to proposed status for operator
32	// attention. It enforces OperatorRequired=true and the canonical Reason and
33	// Source from the ParkReason mapping. The mutate callback runs after the
34	// status transition; pass nil if no additional metadata is needed.
35	func (s *Store) ParkToProposed(id string, reason ParkReason, mutate func(*Bead)) error {
36		meta, ok := parkReasonMetaMap[reason]
37		if !ok {
38			return fmt.Errorf("bead: unknown ParkReason %q", reason)
39		}
40		return s.TransitionLifecycle(id, StatusProposed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
41			OperatorRequired: true,
42			Reason:           meta.Reason,
43			Source:           meta.Source,
44		}, func(b *Bead) error {
45			if mutate != nil {
46				mutate(b)
47			}
48			return nil
49		})
50	}

26	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
27	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
28	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
29	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31	// returned to a caller.
32	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
33		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
34		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
35		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
36		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
37		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
38		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA — the candidate revision
39		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
40		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
41		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
42		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
43		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
46		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
47		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
48		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
49	
50		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
51		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
52		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
53		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
54		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
55		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
56		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
57		// OrchestratorStatus is populated after the landing/pre-merge orchestration
58		// decision so result.json can distinguish worker success from final
59		// non-landing outcomes.
60		OrchestratorStatus string `json:"orchestrator_status,omitempty"`

// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.

## AC-Check Ratification

When an <ac-check> section is present, ratify the mechanical results rather
than re-verifying them independently from the diff:

- result="pass": confirm the evidence is credible. Override to fail only if
  the evidence is fabricated — include judgment_override_reason and a diff
  citation (file:line) in the per_ac evidence string.
- result="fail": mechanically verified failure. Grade as fail and BLOCK unless
  the commit message contains an explicit AC-Waive trailer for this AC.
- result="needs_judgment": adjudicate from the diff. If you cannot determine
  pass/fail without additional bead context from the operator, use
  REQUEST_CLARIFICATION for that AC item.
- result="error": treat as needs_judgment.

Overriding a mechanical grade (pass→fail or fail→pass) requires an explicit
judgment_override_reason note and a concrete diff citation in the evidence.

## Strictness Mode

The <strictness-mode> tag specifies per-bead evidence requirements:

- strict (kind:fix, kind:feat): each AC must be anchored to a named Test*
  function or a diff-touched symbol; file-only evidence is insufficient.
- behavior-light (kind:refactor, kind:chore): build green plus file/symbol
  evidence suffices; test-name match required only when an AC explicitly
  names a Test* function.
- mechanical (kind:doc, kind:mechanical): file presence, renames, or symbol
  evidence only; no test-name or runtime evidence required.

## Verdicts

For each acceptance-criteria (AC) item, decide whether it is implemented
correctly, then assign one overall verdict:

- APPROVE — every AC item is fully and correctly implemented.
- REQUEST_CHANGES — some AC items are partial or have fixable minor issues.
- BLOCK — at least one AC item is not implemented or incorrectly implemented;
  or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
- REQUEST_CLARIFICATION — you cannot adjudicate one or more needs_judgment AC
  items without operator clarification. Use this ONLY when the item is
  ambiguous even given the full diff. This verdict does NOT block the queue;
  it routes to the operator lane for input.

## Required output format (schema_version: 1)

Respond with EXACTLY one JSON object as your final response, fenced as a single ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` code block. Do not include any prose outside the fenced block. The JSON must match this schema:

` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "verdict": "APPROVE",
  "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification",
  "per_ac": [
    { "number": 1, "item": "acceptance criterion text", "grade": "pass", "evidence": "file:line or test evidence" }
  ],
  "findings": [
    { "severity": "info", "summary": "what is wrong or notable", "location": "path/to/file.go:42" }
  ]
}
` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `

Rules:
- "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK", "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION".
- "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
- Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
- Do not echo this template back. Do not write the verdict value anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.`

94			slotResult, slotErr := r.reviewGroupSlot(ctx, b, impl, resultRev, built, artifacts, reviewHarness, reviewRouteLabel, slotRuntime, caps.MaxPromptBytes)
95			slot := ReviewGroupSlotResult{
96				ReviewerIndex: reviewerIndex,
97				Runtime:       slotRuntime,
98			}
99			if slotResult != nil {
100				slotResult.ReviewerIndex = reviewerIndex
101				slot.Result = slotResult
102			}
103			if slotErr != nil {
104				slot.Error = slotErr.Error()
105				if firstErr == nil {
106					firstErr = slotErr
107				}
108			}
109			out.Slots = append(out.Slots, slot)
110		}
111	
112		return out, firstErr
113	}
114	
115	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewGroupSlot(ctx context.Context, b *bead.Bead, impl ImplementerRouting, resultRev string, built BuildReviewPromptResult, artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, reviewHarness, reviewModel string, runtime AgentRunRuntime, maxPromptBytes int) (*ReviewResult, error) {
116		prompt := built.Prompt
117		if built.Overflow {
118			return &ReviewResult{
119				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
120				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
121				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
122				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
123				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
124				ResultRev:       resultRev,
125				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
126				CostUSD:         0,
127				InputBytes:      len(prompt),
128				OutputBytes:     0,
129			}, fmt.Errorf("review-group: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)", len(prompt), maxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
130		}
131	
132		start := time.Now()
133		runRuntime := runtime
134		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
135		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
136		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
137	
138		if runErr != nil {
139			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
140				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
141				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
142				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
143				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
144				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
145				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
146				ResultRev:       resultRev,
147				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
148				DurationMS:      durationMS,
149				CostUSD:         resultCost(result),
150				InputBytes:      len(prompt),
151				OutputBytes:     0,
152			}
153			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("review-group: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
154		}
155	
156		actualHarness := reviewHarness
157		actualModel := reviewModel
158		actualProvider := ""
159		actualPower := 0
160		if result != nil {
161			if result.Harness != "" {
162				actualHarness = result.Harness
163			}
164			if result.Model != "" {
165				actualModel = result.Model
166			}
167			actualProvider = result.Provider
168			actualPower = result.ActualPower
169			durationMS = result.DurationMS
170		}
171	
172		output := ""
173		sessionID := ""
174		if result != nil {
175			output = result.Output
176			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
177		}
178		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
179		var strictVerdict Verdict
180		var rationale string
181		var findings []Finding
182		if parseErr == nil {
183			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
184			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
185			findings = parsed.Findings
186			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
187				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
188				for _, f := range findings {
189					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
190					if line == "" {
191						continue
192					}
193					if f.Location != "" {
194						line = f.Location + ": " + line
195					}
196					parts = append(parts, line)
197				}
198				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
199			}
200		}
201		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
202		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
203			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
204			Rationale:        rationale,
205			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
206			Findings:         findings,
207			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
208			RawOutput:        output,
209			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
210			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
211			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
212			SessionID:        sessionID,
213			BaseRev:          baseRev,
214			ResultRev:        resultRev,
215			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
216			DurationMS:       durationMS,
217			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
218			InputBytes:       len(prompt),
219			OutputBytes:      len(output),
220		}
221	
222		if parseErr != nil {
223			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
224			if strings.TrimSpace(output) == "" {
225				class = evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
226			}
227			reviewRes.Error = class
228			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("review-group: %s: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", class, parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
229		}
230		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
231			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
232				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
233				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
234				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower, resultRev),
235				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
236				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
237			})
238		}
239		return reviewRes, nil
240	}

26	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
27	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
28	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
29	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31	// returned to a caller.
32	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
33		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
34		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
35		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
36		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
37		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
38		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA — the candidate revision
39		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
40		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
41		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
42		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
43		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
46		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
47		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
48		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
49	
50		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
51		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
52		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
53		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
54		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
55		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
56		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
57		// OrchestratorStatus is populated after the landing/pre-merge orchestration
58		// decision so result.json can distinguish worker success from final
59		// non-landing outcomes.
60		OrchestratorStatus string `json:"orchestrator_status,omitempty"`
61	
62		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
63		Reason      string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
64		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
65		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
66		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.

168	var RunStateRefreshInterval = 10 * time.Second

170	// ExecuteBeadRuntime carries the non-durable plumbing for an execute-bead
171	// run: per-invocation intent (FromRev, PromptFile, WorkerID) and
172	// non-serializable injection seams (BeadEvents, Service, AgentRunner).
173	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
174	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed
175	// via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's rcfg argument.
176	//
177	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
178	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
179		FromRev         string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
180		PromptFile      string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
181		Output          io.Writer
182		WorkerID        string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
183		BeadEvents      BeadEventAppender
184		BeadCancel      BeadCancelStore // optional: enables operator-cancel mid-attempt poll
185		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
186		Service         agentlib.FizeauService
187		AgentRunner     AgentRunner
188		// RateLimitMaxWait bounds the per-bead total wait spent on rate-limit
189		// retries (ddx-c6e3db02 RateLimitRetryContract / TD-031 §8.4). Zero uses
190		// RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget (5 min). Negative disables the wrapper —
191		// rate-limit responses fall through to the standard execution_failed path.
192		RateLimitMaxWait time.Duration
193	}
194	
195	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
196	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
197	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
198	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
199	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
200	// worktree.
201	type GitOps interface {
202		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
203		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
204		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
205		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
206		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
207		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
208		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
209		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
210		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
211		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
212		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
213		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
214		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
215		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
216		// other work that might prune it.
217		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
218		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
219		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
220		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
221	}
222	
223	// AgentRunner runs an agent with the given options.
224	type AgentRunner interface {
225		Run(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error)
226	}

1	// Package accheck mechanically verifies acceptance criteria against the
2	// working tree and diff. It is invoked by `ddx bead ac-check <id>` to produce
3	// a structured per-AC result file that both implementer (self-check
4	// pre-commit) and reviewer (gate ratification) consume.
5	//
6	// Per ddx-c739d7ec: the tool classifies each AC line into a kind
7	// (test-name | build-gate | negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) then
8	// resolves evidence mechanically. Prose AC always yields needs_judgment;
9	// never auto-pass, never auto-fail.
10	package accheck
11	
12	import (
13		"bufio"
14		"fmt"
15		"io"
16		"os/exec"
17		"path/filepath"
18		"regexp"
19		"strings"
20	)
21	
22	// SchemaVersion is bumped when the output JSON shape changes incompatibly.
23	const SchemaVersion = 1
24	
25	// Kind classifies an AC line by the evidence we know how to gather for it.
26	type Kind string
27	
28	const (
29		KindTestName   Kind = "test-name"
30		KindBuildGate  Kind = "build-gate"
31		KindNegative   Kind = "negative"
32		KindSymbol     Kind = "symbol"
33		KindMechanical Kind = "mechanical"
34		KindProse      Kind = "prose"
35	)
36	
37	// Result is the verdict on one AC item.
38	type Result string
39	
40	const (
41		ResultPass          Result = "pass"
42		ResultFail          Result = "fail"
43		ResultNeedsJudgment Result = "needs_judgment"
44		ResultError         Result = "error"
45	)
46	
47	// Item represents one parsed AC line with its classification.
48	type Item struct {
49		AC      int    // 1-based AC number
50		Text    string // original AC text (single line)
51		Kind    Kind   // classified kind
52		Name    string // extracted entity (test name, symbol, etc.); empty for prose
53		Alias   string // operator-declared substitution (e.g., TestFoo -> TestFoo/bar)
54	}
55	
56	// Output is the structured per-AC verification output written to
57	// .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json.
58	type Output struct {
59		SchemaVersion int     `json:"schema_version"`
60		BeadID        string  `json:"bead_id"`
61		AttemptID     string  `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
62		Summary       Summary `json:"summary"`
63		Items         []Entry `json:"items"`
64	}
65	
66	// Summary aggregates counts across items.
67	type Summary struct {
68		Pass          int `json:"pass"`
69		Fail          int `json:"fail"`
70		NeedsJudgment int `json:"needs_judgment"`
71		Error         int `json:"error"`
72	}
73	
74	// Entry is one per-AC result in the structured output.
75	type Entry struct {
76		AC       int    `json:"ac"`
77		Kind     Kind   `json:"kind"`
78		Name     string `json:"name,omitempty"`
79		Result   Result `json:"result"`
80		Evidence string `json:"evidence"`

19	// NowFunc allows tests to override time.Now for deterministic PreserveRef output.
20	var NowFunc = time.Now
21	
22	// PreserveRef builds the documented hidden ref for a preserved iteration.
23	func PreserveRef(beadID, baseRev string) string {
24		shortSHA := baseRev
25		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
26			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
27		}
28		timestamp := NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
29		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, timestamp, shortSHA)
30	}
31	
32	// GateCheckResult records the outcome of one required execution gate.
33	type GateCheckResult struct {
34		DefinitionID string `json:"definition_id"`
35		Required     bool   `json:"required"`
36		ExitCode     int    `json:"exit_code"`
37		// Status is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
38		Status string `json:"status"`
39		Stdout string `json:"stdout,omitempty"`
40		Stderr string `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
41		// Ratchet captures the ratchet evaluation when the gate declared
42		// thresholds. Populated for both pass and fail decisions so HELIX can
43		// distinguish ratchet outcomes from generic command failures.
44		Ratchet *RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// RatchetEvidence is the machine-readable record of one ratchet evaluation
48	// performed before landing. Threshold/Observed carry the authored ratchet
49	// and the observed value; Decision is "pass" or "fail"; Reason provides a
50	// short explanation of what was compared ("observed 310 ms > ratchet 250 ms").
51	type RatchetEvidence struct {
52		DefinitionID string  `json:"definition_id"`
53		MetricID     string  `json:"metric_id,omitempty"`
54		Comparison   string  `json:"comparison,omitempty"`

60	}
61	
62	// RatchetPreserveReason marks a landing that was preserved because a declared
63	// ratchet was not met. Callers match on this exact string (via the landing
64	// result's Reason field) to bucket ratchet-preserved attempts apart from
65	// generic gate failures.
66	const RatchetPreserveReason = "ratchet miss"
67	
68	// executeBeadChecks is the machine-readable schema for checks.json.
69	// Written by the orchestrator when gate evaluation runs.
70	type executeBeadChecks struct {
71		AttemptID       string            `json:"attempt_id"`
72		EvaluatedAt     time.Time         `json:"evaluated_at"`
73		Summary         string            `json:"summary"`
74		Results         []GateCheckResult `json:"results"`
75		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
76		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
77	}
78	
79	// OrchestratorGitOps abstracts the git operations needed by the parent-side
80	// orchestrator for preserving worker results under iteration refs.
81	//
82	// NOTE: The Merge(dir, rev) method that existed here before the land
83	// coordinator redesign has been DELETED. All target-branch writes now flow
84	// through Land() in execute_bead_land.go and its per-project serialized
85	// coordinator. See ddx-8746d8a6 / ddx-e14efc58 / ddx-6aa50e57 for the
86	// rationale: the old path produced "chore: checkpoint before merge" noise
87	// and workers racing on the same projectRoot could corrupt each other's
88	// intermediate state. Land() serializes through a single goroutine and
89	// uses `git merge --no-ff` when the target has advanced, so the worker's
90	// commit is never rewritten and replay sees the same inputs it originally saw.
91	//
92	// The LandingAdvancer field on BeadLandingOptions is the coordinator
93	// injection point for LandBeadResult callers that need to ff the target
94	// branch. When LandingAdvancer is nil (the interactive single-bead CLI),
95	// LandBeadResult falls back to preserving the result under
96	// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... rather than modifying the target branch.
97	type OrchestratorGitOps interface {
98		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
99	}
100	
101	// RealOrchestratorGitOps implements OrchestratorGitOps via os/exec git commands.
102	type RealOrchestratorGitOps struct{}
103	
104	// UpdateRef updates a git ref to point at sha.
105	func (r *RealOrchestratorGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
106		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
107		if err != nil {
108			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
109		}
110		return nil
111	}
112	
113	// BeadLandingOptions controls how the orchestrator lands a completed worker result.
114	type BeadLandingOptions struct {
115		// NoMerge skips the land step and preserves the result under
116		// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... instead.
117		NoMerge bool
118	
119		// WtPath is the path to a worktree checked out at ResultRev, used for gate
120		// evaluation. When empty, gate evaluation is skipped (the worktree has
121		// typically been cleaned up by the time the orchestrator runs).
122		WtPath string
123	
124		// GovernIDs are the governing artifact IDs to use for gate evaluation.
125		// Only used when WtPath is non-empty. Typically extracted from the worker's
126		// manifest artifact via ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest.
127		GovernIDs []string
128	
129		// ChecksArtifactPath is the absolute path to write checks.json. Optional.
130		ChecksArtifactPath string
131		// ChecksArtifactRel is the relative path stored in the result for checks.json.
132		ChecksArtifactRel string
133	
134		// LandingAdvancer, when non-nil, replaces the old in-process Merge step
135		// with the coordinator-pattern Land() call. The callback is expected to
136		// run fetch → (ff or merge) → push serialized against other submissions
137		// for the same projectRoot. When nil, LandBeadResult falls back to
138		// preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/...
139		// rather than touching the target branch.
140		LandingAdvancer func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error)
141	}
142	
143	// BeadLandingResult records the outcome of the orchestrator's landing step.
144	type BeadLandingResult struct {
145		// Outcome is one of: "merged", "preserved", "no-changes".
146		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
147		// Reason is a human-readable explanation of the outcome.
148		Reason string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
149		// PreserveRef is set when the result was preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/...
150		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`

data.output:
150		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
151		// GateResults holds the outcome of each required execution gate.
152		GateResults []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
153		// RequiredExecSummary is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
154		RequiredExecSummary string `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
155		// ChecksFile is the relative path to checks.json when gate results were written.
156		ChecksFile string `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
157		// RatchetEvidence aggregates every ratchet-evaluated gate's evidence for
158		// the attempt. Surfaced separately from GateResults so HELIX and other
159		// consumers can tell apart ratchet outcomes from generic gate failures.
160		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
161		// RatchetSummary is "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
162		RatchetSummary string `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
163	}
164	
165	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
166	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
167	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
168	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
169	//
170	// Outcome rules:
171	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
172	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
173	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
174	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
175	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
176	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
177		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
178	
179		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
180		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
181		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
182		agentFailed := res.ExitCode != 0 || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
183		if agentFailed && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
184			landing.Outcome = "error"
185			switch {
186			case res.Reason != "":
187				landing.Reason = res.Reason
188			case res.Error != "":
189				landing.Reason = res.Error
190			default:
191				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
192			}
193			return landing, nil
194		}
195	
196		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
197		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
198			if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence || res.FailureMode == FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced {
199				landing.Outcome = "no-evidence"
200				if res.Reason != "" {
201					landing.Reason = res.Reason
202				} else if res.Error != "" {
203					landing.Reason = res.Error
204				} else {
205					landing.Reason = "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt"
206				}
207				return landing, nil
208			}
209			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
210			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
211				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
212			} else {
213				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
214			}
215			return landing, nil
216		}
217	
218		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
219		if agentFailed {
220			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
221			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
222				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
223			}
224			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
225			landing.PreserveRef = ref
226			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
227			return landing, nil
228		}
229	
230		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
231		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
232		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting
233		// through the coordinator. It mutates res.GateResults / RequiredExecSummary
234		// / RatchetEvidence / RatchetSummary / ChecksFile in place; we mirror the
235		// outputs onto landing so ApplyLandingToResult can copy them back later
236		// without losing the existing landing.* contract.
237		anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gateErr := EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(
238			opts.WtPath, opts.GovernIDs, res, projectRoot,
239			opts.ChecksArtifactPath, opts.ChecksArtifactRel,
240		)
241		if gateErr != nil {
242			return nil, gateErr
243		}
244		gateResults := res.GateResults
245		landing.GateResults = gateResults
246		landing.RequiredExecSummary = res.RequiredExecSummary
247		landing.RatchetEvidence = res.RatchetEvidence
248		landing.RatchetSummary = res.RatchetSummary
249		landing.ChecksFile = res.ChecksFile
250	
251		// Gate failed: preserve instead of merging. Ratchet misses get a
252		// dedicated reason so status/failure_mode classifiers can distinguish
253		// them from generic command failures.
254		if anyGateFailed {
255			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
256			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
257				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
258			}
259			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
260			landing.PreserveRef = ref
261			if anyRatchetFailed {
262				landing.Reason = RatchetPreserveReason
263			} else {
264				landing.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
265			}
266			return landing, nil
267		}
268	
269		// --no-merge: preserve unconditionally.
270		if opts.NoMerge {
271			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
272			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
273				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
274			}
275			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
276			landing.PreserveRef = ref
277			landing.Reason = "--no-merge specified"
278			return landing, nil
279		}
280	
281		// Default: land the worker's commits on the target branch. When a
282		// LandingAdvancer is provided (server coordinator / --local coordinator)
283		// it runs the fetch → (ff or merge) → push sequence serialized per
284		// projectRoot. When no advancer is provided, LandBeadResult falls back
285		// to preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/ — the interactive
286		// single-bead CLI path, which intentionally does NOT auto-advance the
287		// target branch (the operator moves the ref themselves).
288		if opts.LandingAdvancer != nil {
289			land, landErr := opts.LandingAdvancer(res)
290			if landErr != nil {
291				return nil, fmt.Errorf("land advancer: %w", landErr)
292			}
293			switch land.Status {
294			case "landed":
295				landing.Outcome = "merged"
296				if land.Merged {
297					landing.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
298				}
299				if land.NewTip != "" {
300					res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
301				}
302				if land.PushFailed {
303					landing.Reason = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
304				}
305			case "preserved":
306				landing.Outcome = "preserved"
307				landing.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
308				landing.Reason = land.Reason
309			case "no-changes":
310				landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
311				landing.Reason = land.Reason
312			default:
313				landing.Outcome = "preserved"
314				landing.Reason = "unknown land status: " + land.Status
315			}
316			return landing, nil
317		}
318	
319		// No advancer: preserve under refs/ddx/iterations/ as a safe fallback.
320		ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
321		if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
322			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref (no land advancer): %w", err)
323		}
324		landing.Outcome = "preserved"
325		landing.PreserveRef = ref
326		landing.Reason = "no land advancer configured"
327		return landing, nil
328	}
329	
330	// ApplyLandingToResult merges a BeadLandingResult's fields into an
331	// ExecuteBeadResult so callers can output a single unified record. It
332	// overwrites Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults,
333	// RequiredExecSummary, and ChecksFile based on the landing decision.
334	func ApplyLandingToResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, landing *BeadLandingResult) {
335		res.Outcome = landing.Outcome
336		res.Reason = landing.Reason
337		res.PreserveRef = landing.PreserveRef
338		res.GateResults = landing.GateResults
339		res.RequiredExecSummary = landing.RequiredExecSummary
340		res.ChecksFile = landing.ChecksFile
341		res.RatchetEvidence = landing.RatchetEvidence
342		res.RatchetSummary = landing.RatchetSummary
343		// Re-classify status based on landing outcome and reason.
344		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(landing.Outcome, res.ExitCode, landing.Reason)
345		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, landing.Reason, res.Error)
346		// Refine failure_mode with landing-level signals. A clean merge clears
347		// the field; merge conflict or gate failure overrides the worker-level
348		// classification; other preserved outcomes keep the worker's mode.
349		res.FailureMode = classifyLandingFailureMode(landing.Outcome, landing.Reason, landing.GateResults, res.FailureMode)
